column2: the shop list and the menu generator.

column1: # Values are PER ONE UNIT of the stated unit (multiply by qty). Micros are rough estimates.
# pantry: 1 = on hand. base: 1 = can START a plate. green/probiotic: functional flags. role: for recipe phrasing (base/protein/veg/green/aromatic/sour/seed/fruit/condiment/dairy).
# topper: 1 = a micro-layer item the menu generator may ADD to any slot to repair a nutrient gap (seeds/greens/sours/moringa).

column2: and only one serving per day — that is what keeps a repair edible.

column1: # shelf = days it keeps AFTER buying — decides the shopping run (<=3 fresh/daily · 4-14 weekly · >14 monthly staple).
#         it also caps how far ahead you can buy: you cannot stock a month of spinach.
# pack  = how many of `unit` come in one purchase pack. packname = what you actually ask for at the shop.
#         CAREFUL: for dals/grains `unit` is a COOKED cup but you buy a DRY packet. Convert
#         g_dry_per_cup = kcal_per_cup / (kcal_per_g_dry)  -> 1 cup cooked dal = ~35 g dry
#         Getting this wrong silently multiplies every buy (a 12-cup dal pack over-buys 2.3x).
#         all of these are estimates — tune them and re-run `python3 app.py import-csv`.
# lead = HOURS of ahead-time needed BEFORE you can cook with this (soaking
#   `effort` is cook time; `lead` is the thing effort misses. A 12-min rajma bowl is a fiction
#   if nobody soaked the rajma last night. The menu page turns this into a nightly prep queue.
# prep = what you actually do. No commas.
# makes = space-sep raw input ids this row is MADE from (soaking/sprouting/bhuna). Presence marks a
#   PREP OUTPUT — a thing you make
column2: not buy. It gets its own stock + freshness clock (shelf). Marking a

column1: #   prep "done" on index/menu bumps this row's stock; it then expires on its own shelf-life clock.
# needform/gated = the FLAG escape hatch for effects that are real but unquantified (forms.csv
#   mechanism=matrix/fat). needform = the form.csv id that must be applied; gated = the nutrients
#   that should NOT be credited until it is. A flag not a multiplier: the planner WARNS
#   silently scales. Whole til really does deliver far less calcium than ground til - declining to
#   invent the percentage is not the same as claiming the effect is zero.
# keep = 1 = a STANDING prep you always want a batch of ready — the prep queue nags when its stock is empty.
# --- EXPANSION (2026-07-19): rows below generated from ICMR-NIN IFCT 2017 via the `ifct2017` npm
# --- package - real per-100g composition scaled to the unit here. Energy from enerc (kJ -> kcal); the
# --- oils/fats table carries no enerc so those are 9 kcal/g of fat. vita = retinol + carotene-eq/12 (ug RAE).
# --- Non-nutrient columns (pantry/pack/shelf/prep) are still hand-set estimates - tune them.
# --- Known IFCT gaps: it measures no retinol for dairy so milk/paneer vitA reads low; B12 is not a column at all.
# --- COVERAGE PASS: added by enumerating IFCT groups rather than by recall. Numbers are all
# --- 0 here on purpose — `python3 app.py ifct-derive --write` fills them from the table.
# --- PREP OUTPUTS: things you MAKE not buy. `makes` links each to the raw ids it comes from; marking
# --- it done (index/menu prep queue) bumps its stock and starts the shelf-life clock. sprout is one too.
